Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The European AllSky7 fireball network 104 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 954 upvotes, 196 comments
  3. DOOM Over DNS 331 upvotes, 87 comments
  4. QRV Operating System: QNX on RISC-V 44 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Meow.camera 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Show HN: I put an AI agent on a $7/month VPS with IRC as its transport layer 307 upvotes, 88 comments
  7. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 928 upvotes, 320 comments
  8. Local Bernstein theory, and lower bounds for Lebesgue constants 39 upvotes, 7 comments
  9. I am leaving the AI party after one drink 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. Sand from Different Beaches in the World 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 414 upvotes, 153 comments
  12. Installing a Let's Encrypt TLS Certificate on a Brother Printer with Certbot 79 upvotes, 22 comments
  13. How and why to take a logarithm of an image [video] 120 upvotes, 43 comments
  14. $500 GPU outperforms Claude Sonnet on coding benchmarks 432 upvotes, 233 comments
  15. Gzip decompression in 250 lines of Rust 55 upvotes, 22 comments
  16. Iran-linked hackers have breached FBI Director Kash Patel's personal emails 22 upvotes, 6 comments
  17. Desk for people who work at home with a cat 79 upvotes, 37 comments
  18. Apple says no one using Lockdown Mode has been hacked with spyware 26 upvotes, 9 comments
  19. A Faster Alternative to Jq 298 upvotes, 183 comments
  20. Anatomy of the .claude/ Folder 134 upvotes, 78 comments
  21. The 'paperwork flood': How I drowned a bureaucrat before dinner 395 upvotes, 308 comments
  22. Hold on to Your Hardware 417 upvotes, 347 comments
  23. EMachines never obsolete PCs: More than a meme 23 upvotes, 11 comments
  24. Schedule tasks on the web 240 upvotes, 205 comments
  25. Apple discontinues the Mac Pro 570 upvotes, 524 comments
  26. We rewrote JSONata with AI in a day, saved $500k/year 242 upvotes, 224 comments
  27. People inside Microsoft are fighting to drop mandatory Microsoft Account 118 upvotes, 101 comments
  28. Hong Kong Police Can Now Demand Phone Passwords Under New Security Rules 109 upvotes, 95 comments
  29. The Last Gasps of the Rent Seeking Class 47 upvotes, 37 comments
  30. Rank the 50 best Apple products 37 upvotes, 29 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 28 March 2026. Source here.
